positions.label: South wing toward the lower Don
Kessel south sector; passed to 6. Armee

sources.citation: Glantz, D. & House, J. — Stalingrad trilogy (To the Gates of Stalingrad; Armageddon in Stalingrad; Endgame at Stalingrad), 2009–2014
Niehorster, L. — German 6th Army order of battle, 19 Nov 1942

de-h-ak-4.notes: Demonstrates a temporal parent change: advanced under 4. Panzerarmee, encircled under 6. Armee (subordination switched as the Kessel formed).

sources.citation: Niehorster, L. — German 6th Army order of battle, 19 Nov 1942
Glantz, D. & House, J. — Stalingrad trilogy (To the Gates of Stalingrad; Armageddon in Stalingrad; Endgame at Stalingrad), 2009–2014

de-h-ak-51.notes: Pilot coverage: Case Blue to the surrender. Commanded by Seydlitz-Kurzbach during the city fighting.

sources.citation: Glantz, D. & House, J. — Stalingrad trilogy (To the Gates of Stalingrad; Armageddon in Stalingrad; Endgame at Stalingrad), 2009–2014
Niehorster, L. — German 6th Army order of battle, 19 Nov 1942

de-h-id-113.notes: Pilot: corps assignment held constant over the pilot window; positions derived from the corps sector (approximate).

su-front-stalingrad.notes: Scaffold (no positions yet). The Aug–Sep 1942 Stalingrad/Southeastern Front reshuffle is simplified to a single front for the pilot.

sources.citation: Glantz, D. & House, J. — Stalingrad trilogy (To the Gates of Stalingrad; Armageddon in Stalingrad; Endgame at Stalingrad), 2009–2014

su-gd-rd-37.notes: Formed from the 1st Airborne Corps. Pilot: city positions schematic (west-bank strip); the division held a narrow riverside sector inside the drawn pocket ring, so side checks report it as contested.
